title: Savory Velvety Pork Stir-fry
description: A one-pot Brazilian dish featuring savory pork in a velvety sauce made with dry sherry and blanched vegetables for added flavor and low-carb appeal.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Cut pork into bite-sized pieces, season with salt and pepper, and add to the skillet. Cook for 4-5 minutes on each side or until browned and cooked through.
Remove pork from skillet and set aside.
Add cherry tomatoes and celery stalks to the skillet. Sauté for about 2 minutes until softened.
Return pork to the skillet, pour in dry sherry, and stir-fry for another minute allowing the wine to reduce slightly.
Top with bell peppers, tossing well to combine all ingredients into a velvety sauce. Cook for an additional 3-4 minutes or until peppers are tender.
Serve hot as a main course.
